title: PhyX 开放题
rewritten_question_text: In the traction apparatus shown in the figure, a patient with a dislocated shoulder has two pulls, \(\vec{A}\) and \(\vec{B}\), of equal magnitude. If they must combine to produce an outward traction force of 12.8 N on the patient's arm, what should the magnitude of each pull be?

title: PhyX 开放题
rewritten_question_text: All of the stars of the Big Dipper may appear to be the same distance from Earth, but in fact they are very far from each other. The figure shows the distances from Earth to each of these stars. The distances are given in light-years (ly), the distance that light travels in one year. One light-year equals $9.461 \times 10^{15} m$. To an inhabitant of a planet orbiting Merak, how many degrees apart in the sky would Alkaid and our Sun be?
